seoTitle: Aishi Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors Product Catalog | LiTong Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Aishi aluminum electrolytic capacitors, radial lead, snap-in, screw terminal, and solid polymer capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and alternative parts.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Aishi capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider voltage rating (1.2-1.5x operating voltage), capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, physical size, and lifetime requirements. Aishi offers comprehensive selection guides for each product series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized Aishi capacitor selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Aishi's radial and snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors have wire leads for through-hole mounting and are suitable for lower power applications. Snap-in capacitors have PCB mounting terminals, higher capacitance values, and are designed for high-power applications like SMPS and LED drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use radial capacitors for consumer electronics and low-power applications. Use snap-in capacitors for power supplies above 100W and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What are Aishi's screw terminal capacitors used for?
faqs.answer: Screw terminal capacitors are designed for high-power applications requiring large capacitance values and high ripple current capability. Common applications include inverters, welding equipment, UPS systems, and industrial motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications above 500W or requiring capacitance above 10,000uF, consider screw terminal capacitors for reliable connections.

faqs.question: Are Aishi capacitors RoHS compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Aishi capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. Aishi also provides halogen-free options for environmentally sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For RoHS compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your specific part numbers.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Aishi capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard Aishi capacitors typically have 4-6 weeks lead time. High-volume or custom specifications may require 8-10 weeks. LiTong Electronics maintains inventory of popular Aishi models for faster delivery.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our stock availability or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

categories.name: Radial Lead Capacitors
categories.description: Standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitors for general-purpose applications
categories.longDescription: Aishi radial lead capacitors are the industry standard for general-purpose aluminum electrolytic capacitors. These capacitors feature wire leads for through-hole mounting and are available in a wide range of capacitance and voltage ratings. Ideal for consumer electronics, power supplies, and industrial applications where compact size and cost-effectiveness are important. Contact LiTong Electronics, your authorized Aishi distributor, for radial capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, size constraints, and lifetime requirements. RS series for standard, RH for high temperature, RT for low impedance.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors are used in consumer electronics, power supplies, audio equipment, LED drivers, and industrial controls. They are ideal for applications requiring compact size and cost-effective solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For general-purpose applications with moderate capacitance requirements, radial capacitors offer the best cost-performance ratio.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RS, RH, and RT series?
faqs.answer: RS series is the standard radial capacitor for general applications. RH series offers higher temperature ratings (up to 125C) and longer lifetime. RT series features low impedance for high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RS series for standard applications up to 85C. Use RH series for high-temperature applications. Use RT series for switching power supplies.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard radial capacitors are rated for 2,000 to 10,000 hours at 85C or 105C. Lifetime can be extended significantly with proper derating and lower operating temperatures following the Arrhenius equation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and use voltage derating. Calculate expected lifetime using the Arrhenius equation.

faqs.question: Can Aishi radial capacitors be used in LED driver applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aishi radial capacitors are widely used in LED drivers, particularly for output filtering. Select capacitors with appropriate lifetime ratings and ripple current capability for your LED application.
faqs.decisionGuide: For LED drivers, select capacitors with 8,000+ hour lifetime ratings and sufficient ripple current capability. Consider RH series for high-temperature environments.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current rating of Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ripple current ratings range from 0.1A to 2.5A depending on capacitance, voltage, and case size. Higher capacitance and larger cases typically offer higher ripple current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application requirements for reliable operation.

faqs.question: Are Aishi radial capacitors available in low-profile versions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aishi offers low-profile radial capacitors with reduced height for space-constrained applications. These are ideal for slim LED drivers and compact power supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For height-restricted applications, specify low-profile versions with reduced diameter-to-height ratios.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 1000uF 25V
products.shortDescription: Standard radial capacitor with 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating for general-purpose applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS-1000uF-25V is a standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for general-purpose applications. With 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating, it provides reliable filtering and decoupling for low-voltage circuits.
This capacitor features a compact 10mm diameter case with 5mm lead spacing, making it ideal for space-constrained designs. The standard radial form factor enables easy through-hole mounting on PCBs.

Rated for 2000 hours at 85C, this capacitor is suitable for consumer electronics and industrial applications with standard lifetime requirements. The cost-effective design makes it ideal for high-volume production.
products.faeReview: The RS-1000uF-25V is my standard recommendation for low-voltage filtering applications. The 1000uF/25V rating is perfect for 12V and 5V power supply output filtering. The compact 10mm size fits in most designs, and the 5mm lead spacing is compatible with standard PCB layouts. For consumer electronics where cost is critical, this capacitor offers excellent value. I recommend keeping the operating voltage below 20V for extended lifetime.

alternativeParts.comparison: Voltage: 25V same; Capacitance: 680uF vs 1000uF; Size: same
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 680uF is sufficient

alternativeParts.comparison: Voltage: 25V same; Temperature: 105C vs 85C; Lifetime: 5000h vs 2000h

applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for 5V DC power supply

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the RS-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 1.2A at 85C and 120Hz. This rating ensures reliable operation in power supply applications with moderate switching frequencies. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 1.4A at 65C. For high-frequency applications above 100kHz, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR. Always verify the actual operating temperature and frequency conditions in your application to ensure the capacitor operates within its safe operating area.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 1.2A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the RS-1000uF-25V at typical operating conditions?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85C. Using the Arrhenius equation, we can calculate the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures. At 65C, the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. At 25C ambient, you can achieve up to 128,000 hours. Every 10C reduction in operating temperature approximately doubles the capacitor lifetime. These calculations assume voltage derating to 80% of rated voltage and ripple current within specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum capacitor lifetime, operate at the lowest practical temperature with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations specific to your application conditions.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for reliable operation of the RS-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 25V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 20V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 13V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the RS-1000uF-25V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40C to +85C. At the maximum rated temperature of 85C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the RS-1000uF-25V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.02 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies. For switching power supplies operating at 100kHz, consider the frequency characteristics of ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple RS-1000uF-25V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting RS-1000uF-25V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 2000uF capacitance and approximately 0.010 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 2.4A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 470uF 50V
products.shortDescription: Standard radial capacitor with 470uF capacitance and 50V rating for medium-voltage applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS-470uF-50V is a standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for medium-voltage applications. With 470uF capacitance and 50V rating, it provides reliable filtering for 24V and 48V power supply circuits.
This capacitor features a 10mm diameter case with standard 5mm lead spacing. The optimized electrode design provides good ripple current capability for its size.
Rated for 2000 hours at 85C, this capacitor is suitable for industrial and consumer applications. The standard form factor ensures compatibility with existing designs.
products.faeReview: The RS-470uF-50V is ideal for 24V industrial power supplies. The 470uF value provides good filtering for medium-power applications. I often use this capacitor in PLC power supplies and industrial control systems. The 50V rating provides good margin for 24V and 48V systems.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 330uF is sufficient

applicationScenarios.description: Bulk capacitance for programmable logic controller
